zh.lifeSummary: Johanna Holland是Joseph Holland和Unknown 1st wife of Joseph Holland的女兒，於大約1617年出生在英國倫敦。她於大約1643年與John Perry在英國結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1683年9月9日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可沃特托享年67歲，埋葬在美國麻薩諸塞州米德萊可沃特托笛卡爾 波英 普拉斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Johanna Holland是Joseph Holland和Unknown 1st wife of Joseph Holland的女儿，于大约1617年出生在英国倫敦。她于大约1643年与John Perry在英国结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1683年9月9日在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼米德萊可沃特托享年67岁，埋葬在美国马萨诸塞州米德萊可沃特托笛卡爾 波英 普拉斯。

nameMeanings.name: Holland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, German, Dutch, Danish, Norwegian, Swedish, French, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from Holland, a province of the Netherlands.

English: habitational name from Downholland or Upholland (Lancashire), Hulland (Derbyshire), the Parts of Holland, one of the three administrative subdivisions of Lincolnshire, any of the four places called Hoyland (southern Yorkshire), and possibly Great and Little Holland (Essex). The placenames all derive from Old English hōh ‘heel, spur of land’ + land ‘land’.

English: habitational name either from Hoeland (Farm) in Bury (Sussex), or from Holland's Barn in Albourne (Sussex). The placename in Bury has the same etymology as in 1 above, while the placename in Albourne may derive from Old English hol ‘hole, hollow’ + land ‘land’.

variants.name: Hollands
meanings: English: variant of Holland , especially 3, with post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Howland
meanings: History: Howland was the name of three Quaker brothers, original settlers in Marshfield, MA. They were from Huntingdonshire, England. The eldest, John Howland (c. 1593–1672) was a passenger on the Mayflower, servant to Gov. John Carver, who died in the first winter at Plymouth Colony.

variants.name: Hollander
meanings: Dutch (also Den Hollander), German (also Holländer), English, Swedish, and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name for someone from Holland (see Holland 1).

variants.name: Holand
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Holland  .
Norwegian: habitational name from about twenty farmsteads so named, composed of Old Norse hár ‘high’ + land ‘land, field’, denoting a high-lying farm.

variants.name: Aland
meanings: Scandinavian (Åland): topographic name from the Åland Islands in the Gulf of Bothnia.

English: topographic name from Middle English atte lande (Old French laund) ‘dweller at the glade or pasture’, or sometimes perhaps, ‘dweller at the old cultivated land(s)’ from Old English eald, ald ‘old’ + land ‘land’.

nameMeanings.name: Johanna
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.

variants.name: Jane
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

en.lifeSummary: Johanna Holland was born about 1617, in London, England as the daughter of Joseph Holland and Unknown 1st wife of Joseph Holland. She married John Perry about 1643, in England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 1 daughter. She died on 9 September 1683, in Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 67, and was buried in Old Burying Place, Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts, United States.

ja.lifeSummary: Johanna Hollandは約1617年にイギリスロンドンでJoseph HollandとUnknown 1st wife of Joseph Hollandの娘として生まれた。約1643年にイギリスでJohn Perryと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1683年9月9日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーミッドレセックスワテルトウンで67歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州ミッドレセックスワテルトウンオルド ブリイング プレイスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Johanna Holland(은)는 대략 1617년 영국 런던에서 Joseph Holland(와)과 Unknown 1st wife of Joseph Holland의 딸로 태어났다. 그녀는 대략 1643년 영국에서 John Perry(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1683년 9월 9일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 미들레세스 왓르타운에서 67세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 매사추세츠 주 미들레세스 왓르타운 드 버이잉 플레이스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Johanna Holland nasceu aproximadamente 1617, em London, Inglaterra filha de Joseph Holland e Unknown 1st wife of Joseph Holland. Ela casou-se com John Perry aproximadamente 1643, em Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 9 de setembro de 1683, em Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America, com 67 anos, e foi sepultada em Old Burying Place, Watertown, Middlesex, Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Johanna Holland родилась около 1617 г. в местности Лондон, Англия. Её родителей звали Joseph Holland и Unknown 1st wife of Joseph Holland. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Perry около 1643 г. в местности Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла 9 сентября 1683 г. в местности вэитиртоун, миддлесекс, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 67 лет и была похоронена в местности олд буринг плеис, вэитиртоун, миддлесекс, Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты.
